key_metrics_output: Particle count, polymer type identification, spike recovery (~90%), deposition flux (particles/m²/event or /day)
abstract: Critical assessment of analytical methods for microplastics analysis covering sampling, extraction, and identification techniques including optical microscopy, dye staining, flow cytometry, FT-IR, Raman spectroscopy, and py-GC-MS. The review evaluates methods for limitations, field applicability, and cost-efficiency to establish harmonized standardized operational protocols for microplastics monitoring in drinking water and environmental samples, with consideration of nanoplastic analysis.
state of the currently applied identification and quantification tools for microplastics
naked eye detection, general optical microscopy, dye staining, flow cytometry, FT-Ir and microscopy, Raman spectroscopy and microscopy, py-GC-MS, TED-GCMS
methods were screened for limitations, field work abilities and costs
recommendation for a workflow covering the demands of society, legislation, and administration
